1.question: @spark build the main coupling method4
1.answer:
To build and run a method:

1. Go to Method tab in the sidebar
2. Click '➕ Build Method' button
3. In the dialog, type cycles like:
   • Baseline 5min [ALL]
   • Concentration 2min [A:100nM]
   • Regeneration 30sec [ALL]
4. Click '➕ Add to Method' to build your queue
5. Click '📋 Push to Queue' to add to sidebar
6. Click ▶ Start to begin your method

Tip: Use @spark in the notes field for templates like:
• @spark amine coupling - for surface preparation
• @spark titration - for dose-response
• @spark kinetics - for binding studies

2.question: how do i start an acquisitionn
2.answer:
To start an acquisition:

1. Make sure you're connected (green indicator in Device Status tab)
2. Click the green ▶ Play button in the top toolbar
3. The graph will start showing live data

To stop, click the ⏸ Pause button or ⏹ Stop button.

7.question: where do i save data
7.answer:
To export your data:

1. Click the Export tab in the sidebar
2. Choose your export format (CSV, Excel, JSON)
3. Select which channels to export (A, B, C, D)
4. Click 'Export to File'

Your data will be saved to the export destination folder.

8.question: what are the steps to start an experiment
8.answer:
To start an acquisition:

1. Make sure the device is connected (green Power indicator)
2. Run a calibration if needed (Settings → Calibrate)
3. Click the Start Recording button in the sidebar
4. Data will appear on the Full Sensorgram graph

You can pause or stop the acquisition using the sidebar controls.

9.question: where is my data saved?
9.answer:
To export your data:

1. Click the Export tab in the sidebar
2. Choose your export format (CSV, Excel, JSON)
3. Select which channels to export (A, B, C, D)
4. Click 'Export to File'

Your data will be saved to the export destination folder.
